rationale_top3: BIG4 Emu Beach Holiday Park stands out as an exceptional family destination with world-class facilities that rival the best parks on Australia's east coast. The comprehensive entertainment offering — including a splash water park, two jumping pillows, mini golf, theatre room, nature playground, and recreation room — keeps children entertained for days on end. Parents consistently praise the immaculate grounds, modern amenities, and the park's meticulous maintenance standards, with multiple reviews calling it "the best caravan park" they've experienced across Australia.
rationale_honourable: The park's beachfront location on Emu Beach is a genuine highlight, with direct access paths leading to calm, safe swimming waters and scenic walking trails. Families love falling asleep to the sound of waves and waking to bandicoots and rabbits visiting their sites. The combination of pristine natural environment, spotless facilities, and exceptionally friendly staff creates a holiday experience that has families booking return visits year after year, with many considering it their favourite camping destination in Australia.
water_fun: Splash water park with slides and tipping bucket
kids_play: Two jumping pillows, nature playground, mini golf, theatre
pet_detail: Dog friendly with dedicated tracks to dog beach; pets allowed on sites
best_for: Young families seeking a well-maintained, activity-packed coastal holiday park where kids can play safely all day while parents relax in beautiful natural surroundings.
executive_summary:
PARK: BIG4 Emu Beach Holiday Park
SCORE: 87/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-24
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 232

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (19/20) — Reviews consistently highlight exceptional facilities including splash water park with slides and tipping bucket, two jumping pillows, mini golf, nature playground, theatre room with movies, recreation room with pool tables and ping pong, kids club activities during school holidays, and pedal carts. Families report children entertained "all day" with "hours of entertainment" and "so much to do on site."

Nature (18/20) — Beachfront location with direct access to Emu Beach, calm safe swimming, scenic walking and cycling trails along the coast to Emu Point cafe and Middleton Beach, surrounded by tall peppermint trees and peaceful bushland, wildlife sightings including bandicoots and rabbits, guests love "falling asleep to the sound of waves" with "beautiful beach clean, bluest of blue water."

Cleanliness (15/15) — Overwhelmingly praised as "immaculate," "spotlessly clean," "meticulously maintained" with modern amenities blocks cleaned daily, "some of the nicest ablutions" experienced, camp kitchens well-equipped and clean, grounds beautifully manicured with lush green lawns, staff visibly maintaining throughout the day.

Value (12/15) — Mixed feedback on pricing with several reviews noting "expensive" or "most we have paid in 8 weeks," powered tent sites $150/night, family apartments over $300/night, though many feel facilities justify the cost; some concern about extra charges for mini golf and laundry requiring coins not cards.

Site Size (8/10) — Good-sized sites with concrete slabs and beautiful green grass surrounds, though some reviews mention sites "just too small" or "a little tight" especially for larger vans, laneways can be narrow when busy requiring careful maneuvering, big rig area available with immaculate surroundings.

Sentiment (10/10) — Exceptional positive sentiment with high repeat visitation, families returning year after year calling it their "favourite park over 5 years of caravanning," "100/10" ratings from children, strong recommendations as "number one recommendation for kid friendly caravan parks," visitors "regretted not booking longer" and "will definitely be back."

Location (10/10) — Prime beachfront position barely 100m to beach, 10-15 minutes from Albany CBD, walking distance to Emu Point cafe, short drive to Middleton Beach and major attractions including The Gap and Natural Bridge, Woolworths complex 3km away, peaceful setting yet accessible to everything.

WHY NOT 100:
Some negative staff interactions reported with reception described as "rude and unhelpful" on multiple occasions, impacting customer service experience despite many praising staff friendliness. Value concerns with pricing considered high by some families, particularly budget cabin quality not matching $300+ nightly rates, and additional charges for activities and coin-operated laundry. Site access challenges for larger rigs with narrow internal roads, tight turning circles, and some concrete pads too short for longer caravans requiring leveling blocks.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"best caravan park I've ever stayed at, facilities for the kids are 10/10", "immaculate grounds, 2 jumping pillows, water park, giant playground", "spotlessly clean facilities", "THE BEST place to stay in Albany with kids", "sound of the waves rolling in", "super pet friendly love all the tracks to dog beach", "meticulously maintained", "one of the nicest caravan parks in 9 months of travel", "kids said it was their most favourite park over 5 years", "friendly and helpful staff"

rationale_top3:
King River Palms Caravan Park earns Gold status through its exceptional riverside location and outstanding family-run hospitality. The park's position right on the King River provides free canoes for kayaking and fishing, creating natural entertainment that families consistently highlight as a standout feature. Multiple reviews from families with young children praise the safe environment for bike riding, fishing from the riverbank, and playground access, with one family extending their stay from 2 nights to 5 days because kids loved the activities so much.

What truly elevates this park is the extraordinary customer service from owners Dwayne and Henrietta, who reviewers describe as treating guests "more like friends than guests." The personal touches—from golf cart tours to welcome scones with cream and jam—combined with spotlessly clean amenities and accommodating last-minute bookings during peak periods demonstrate a level of care that resonates strongly with family travellers seeking a relaxed, welcoming atmosphere.
rationale_honourable: The park's outstanding natural setting with established shade trees, abundant birdlife, and direct river frontage creates a peaceful, authentic country experience. Exceptional value pricing at $35-45 per night with no peak period surcharges, plus complimentary canoe use, makes this an affordable family option near Albany.
water_fun: River access with free canoes, kayaking, fishing
kids_play: Small playground, river activities, bike riding area
pet_detail: Dog friendly park with grassed sites; resident Sue may provide pet sitting services for small donation
best_for: Families with young children who prioritize natural outdoor activities, water-based fun, and authentic country hospitality over resort-style amenities.
executive_summary:
PARK: King River Palms Caravan Park
SCORE: 82/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-10
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 122

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (14/20) — Families consistently praised free canoes and kayaks for river use, fishing opportunities right from campsites, small playground, rope swings into river, and safe bike riding areas. Multiple reviews mentioned kids extending stays because they "loved kayaking and fishing" and "played in the playground and in/next to the river." One family noted "dancing birds" as bonus entertainment, though no jumping pillow or waterpark facilities.

Nature (19/20) — Outstanding natural setting directly on King River with "beautiful water frontage," established shade trees, green grassy sites, abundant birdlife including ducks and resident roosters. Reviews repeatedly highlight "right on the river," "beautiful trees and birds," and peaceful natural surrounds. Close proximity to national parks, beaches at Lower King/Nanarup, and 10km to Albany attractions.

Cleanliness (14/15) — Overwhelmingly positive feedback on "spotlessly clean," "immaculate shower areas," "squeaky clean amenities," and well-maintained facilities. Reviews note "possibly the cleanest we've ever found" and owners "kept the park spotless." Minor issues included one uncleaned cabin and occasional lack of soap/toilet paper in cubicles during peak periods.

Value (14/15) — Strong value proposition with "affordable accommodation," pricing at $35-45/night for powered sites, no markup for long weekends/school holidays, and free canoe use. Reviews describe "reasonable price," "fair price," and "good price compared to other parks." One review questioned $45/night value given permanent residents, but majority found excellent value.

Site Size (7/10) — Mixed feedback on site sizing. Positive reviews mention "large grassy sites," "roomy sites," and "big sites" especially for larger rigs. However, multiple reviews note sites can be "very tightly packed in" during busy periods with "everyone parked where they can fit" and even parking spaces used as campsites. River frontage sites are spacious but limited in number.

Sentiment (9/10) — Exceptional guest sentiment with numerous families extending stays, repeat visits ("third time back in last two months"), and strong recommendations. Phrases like "will definitely be back," "home away from home," and "best park we have stayed" dominate. Family-run warmth creates personal connections. Minor detractors from 2-3 negative service experiences.

Location (5/10) — Location 10-15 minutes from Albany town center is convenient but not beachfront. Highway proximity causes road noise for some sites. Tavern across river provides entertainment but also noise on weekends. Good access to attractions (Porongorups, Stirling Range 45min) but requires driving to beaches and amenities.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Limited dedicated kids entertainment infrastructure (small basic playground mentioned needing revamp, no jumping pillow, waterpark, or organized activities that modern family parks offer)
2. Site crowding and inconsistent sizing during peak periods, with some guests squeezed into parking spaces between permanent resident cabins, creating a "trailer park" feel according to some reviews
3. Highway and tavern noise affects some sites, with road trains audible at night and weekend bands playing until midnight, plus limited riverside sites means not all families get the premium river experience

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"Kids loved kayaking and fishing at the river right behind our cabin", "Safe for kids to bike ride", "free canoes for anyone to use", "extended another 3 days", "spotlessly clean", "immaculate shower areas", "treated more like friends than guests", "Beautiful green grass and shady trees", "10/10", "best customer service we have experienced at a caravan park"

rationale_top3: BIG4 Middleton Beach Holiday Park delivers exceptional beachfront location with direct sand-dune access to Middleton Beach, consistently praised as the standout feature by families. The park offers comprehensive entertainment for children including daily kids club with varied activities, heated pool and spa, jumping pillow, two playgrounds, games room with pool and table tennis, and go-kart hire. Families particularly appreciated falling asleep to ocean sounds and the immaculate grounds with spectacular gardens.
rationale_honourable: The park excels in cleanliness and maintenance standards, with spotless amenities, well-kept facilities, and attentive groundskeeping staff regularly mentioned. While the location and facilities score highly, the park loses points on value due to significant pricing concerns raised in recent reviews, with multiple long-term visitors citing 32-55% price increases under new ownership. Site sizes are adequate with concrete slabs but some reviews note challenging caravan manoeuvring in certain spots.
water_fun: Heated pool, spa, netted beach swimming
kids_play: Daily kids club, two playgrounds, jumping pillow
pet_detail: Pets permitted outside peak holiday season with bond required; not allowed during Christmas/peak periods.
best_for: Families with young children who want a full-facility beachfront park with daily organized activities and are willing to pay premium prices for exceptional location and entertainment options.
executive_summary:
PARK: BIG4 Middleton Beach Holiday Park
SCORE: 81/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-15
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 162

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (17/20) — Families consistently praised the daily kids club with different activities, heated pool and spa, two playgrounds, jumping pillow, games room with pool/table tennis/arcade, go-kart hire, and recreation room. Multiple reviews highlighted "entertainment for kids is top notch" and children having "the best time" with "so much for them to do."

Nature (19/20) — Exceptional beachfront location with direct walkway access through sand dunes to Middleton Beach. Guests loved "falling asleep to the sound of the waves," spectacular gardens with "exuberant vegetation," native wildlife including bandicoots, and the "rhythmic lullaby of waves." Walking paths along the boardwalk and proximity to Albany's natural attractions highly praised.

Cleanliness (14/15) — Consistently described as "spotlessly maintained," "immaculate," and "clean as a whistle." Amenities, bathrooms, and cabins received repeated praise for being "impeccable," though a few reviews mentioned older bathroom facilities needing updates and one isolated maintenance issue with locks.

Value (8/15) — Significant concern with recent pricing under new ownership. Multiple long-term guests reported 32-55% price increases, with quotes of $679-$1250 per night for peak season. Reviews frequently mentioned "overpriced," "price gouging," and being "twice that of equal parks," though some acknowledged "you pay for what you get" given the premium location.

Site Size (8/10) — Sites described as "decent size," "spacious," with concrete slabs for vans and grass areas. "Lots of grass" and "roomy" sites noted positively, but some reviews mentioned "incredibly tricky spots" for caravan manoeuvring on outer edges and middle rows when cars parked opposite.

Sentiment (9/10) — Strong repeat visitation with families returning "year after year" and calling it "one of our all-time favourites" and "the best caravan park we've stayed at." High recommendation rates, though recent pricing changes caused some loyal customers to state they "won't be back." Overall sentiment remains very positive with families noting they "can't wait to come back."

Location (10/10) — Perfect score for beachfront position "right on the beach," walking distance to Middleton Beach cafes, tavern, and restaurants, close proximity to Albany town, ANZAC memorial, and regional attractions. Described as "unbeatable" location that "sets the benchmark."

WHY NOT 100:
1. Value concerns are substantial - multiple reviews documented steep price increases of 32-55% under new ownership, with guests calling it "price gouging," "extortion," and stating similar accommodation is "over $200 per night cheaper" elsewhere, making it prohibitive for many families.

2. Security issues raised by several guests noting thefts during peak times "on multiple visits," with the park being unsecured from both main entrance and ocean side, and management reportedly doing little to improve safety with CCTV or locked gates.

3. Some maintenance and facility concerns including bathroom facilities described as "very ordinary" and "very old" needing updates, one major lock issue leaving ladies without amenities access overnight, games room equipment 50% broken with poor maintenance, and inconsistent policies causing a catastrophic dog booking failure that left a family stranded.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"falling asleep to the sound of the waves", "kids club every day with different activities", "price gouging by the new owners", "spotlessly maintained", "entertainment for kids is top notch", "right on the beach", "best caravan park we've stayed at", "thefts happen quite frequently"

rationale_top3: Entertainment facilities are a major highlight with an excellent bounce pillow/jumping cushion that kids love, an 'amazing' pool that's well-maintained, and a rec room offering billiards, table tennis, and basketball - consistently praised across reviews Spacious, well-designed sites with 'very large' drive-through options, concrete slabs for easy setup, and 'plenty of space between sites' making it ideal for larger rigs and families wanting room to spread out Strong family-friendly amenities including exceptionally clean camp kitchens with pizza ovens, good entertainment options for children, and a pet-friendly policy with dedicated pet-friendly cabins available throughout the park
rationale_honourable: Tasman Holiday Parks Albany delivers a solid family-friendly experience with standout features including spacious drive-through sites, excellent on-site entertainment (bounce pillow, pool, rec room), and well-equipped camp kitchens with pizza ovens. While some facilities show their age and occasional security/theft concerns surfaced in reviews, the park's central Albany location, friendly staff, and pet-friendly policies make it a reliable choice for families exploring the region.
water_fun: Pool available, popular with families in summer
kids_play: Bounce pillow, rec room, playground equipment available
pet_detail: Pet-friendly park with pet-friendly cabins and sites available; dogs welcome throughout the park
best_for: This park is best for families with young children who want entertainment on-site (bounce pillow, pool, rec room) while being centrally located to explore Albany's attractions.
executive_summary:
PARK: Tasman Holiday Parks - Albany
SCORE: 72/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-21
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 193

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (16/20) — Excellent feedback on the bounce pillow/jumping cushion which was consistently mentioned as a highlight for kids, pool facilities praised as 'amazing' and well-maintained, rec room with billiards/table tennis/basketball available, though some noise complaints from families near the bounce pillow area

Nature (12/20) — Urban park setting with 'lots of shade', 'lovely grassy and treed sites', 'beautiful trees surrounding the sites', but limited natural bushland or direct nature access; 30-minute walk to city centre, close to beaches and shops but not a true nature-based location

Cleanliness (11/15) — Mixed feedback with many praising 'pristine bathrooms', 'immaculately clean' facilities, 'very clean amenities and grounds', but concerning number of reviews citing dated facilities, inconsistent cleaning (ensuites not cleaned for 14+ days), 'dirty bathrooms', floor cleaning issues, and laundry cleanliness problems

Value (11/15) — Divided opinions: many found it 'well priced', 'great value for money', 'very reasonable price compared to other caravan sites', while others complained it was 'extremely overpriced', '$129 per night expensive for average site', '$90 for tent site inadequate given condition'

Site Size (9/10) — Consistently praised for 'very large sites', 'spacious sites', 'large drive through sites', 'plenty of space between sites', 'lots of space in our lot', with concrete slabs for easy setup, though a few mentioned some tent sites were 'a bit small'

Sentiment (7/10) — Generally positive with many 'will definitely stay again' and 'highly recommend' comments, but sentiment significantly impacted by serious safety concerns (break-in attempts, theft from fridges), dismissive staff responses to safety issues, and inconsistent service experiences

Location (6/10) — Central Albany location praised as 'close to everything', '1km from major retailers', 'walking distance to town' (though 30 minutes), but several reviews noted it's 'a little out of town', 'have to drive to town', and location doesn't offer scenic/beachfront appeal

WHY NOT 100:
1. Significant security and safety concerns with multiple reviews detailing break-in attempts, food theft from communal fridges, and dismissive staff responses to safety incidents, creating serious concerns for families
2. Inconsistent maintenance and cleanliness with dated facilities (especially ensuites), cleaning staff not arriving for days, poor water pressure, washing machines staining clothes, and general maintenance issues in some cabins
3. Value perception issues with pricing considered high by many reviewers ($110-129/night) relative to the dated condition of facilities, lack of natural setting, and urban rather than resort-style experience

rationale_top3: Exceptional waterfront location on Frenchman Bay with stunning views, abundant birdlife including pelicans, and direct water access for a truly peaceful nature-based experience Outstanding value for money with reviewers consistently noting best pricing in Albany for waterfront sites, combined with huge, spacious pitches perfect for large caravans Excellent proximity to Torndirrup National Park (10 minutes) providing easy access to The Gap, Natural Bridge, blowholes, and beautiful coastal walks, while only 10-15 minutes from Albany CBD
rationale_honourable: Super friendly and helpful staff who personally walk guests to their sites and provide exceptional customer service, creating a welcoming atmosphere despite the park's basic facilities and dated infrastructure
water_fun: Waterfront bay swimming, no pool facilities

executive_summary:
PARK: Panorama Caravan Park
SCORE: 68/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-26
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 221

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (6/20) — Reviews confirm no playground, no kids club, no waterpark, and no structured children's activities; one review mentioned a barn with sitting area and kids corner, but entertainment is entirely nature-based with pelicans and bay views providing the main interest for children.

Nature (18/20) — Exceptional natural setting right on the waterfront of Frenchman Bay with stunning water views, pelicans, and abundant birdlife; excellent proximity (10 minutes) to Torndirrup National Park featuring The Gap, Natural Bridge, blowholes, Misery Beach, and coastal walks; reviews consistently praise the peaceful waterfront location and natural beauty.

Cleanliness (11/15) — Mixed evidence with many reviews praising "spotless" and "very clean" amenities that are cleaned daily, but multiple reviews cite dated facilities, dirty camp kitchen ("dirtiest we saw"), insufficient toilets leading to wet floors, and only 3-4 showers total for all guests creating queues during busy periods.

Value (13/15) — Strong value proposition with reviews noting "great value for money," "much better pricing than other Albany parks," "very very good price," and "best price you can find in Albany" for waterfront sites; powered sites noted as cheap even during school holidays.

Site Size (9/10) — Reviews consistently praise "huge camp sites," "large area heeps of room to move," "spacious" sites, "generous sized sites," and waterfront pitches described as "HUGE" with plenty of space; easy drive-through access for large vans.

Sentiment (8/10) — Predominantly positive sentiment with many guests extending stays and returning repeatedly ("been visiting for 10+ years"), though somewhat dampened by organizational issues, mixed facility feedback, and isolated negative experiences with management and permanent residents.

Location (9/10) — Excellent location 10-15 minutes from Albany CBD, perfectly positioned for Torndirrup National Park attractions (less than 10 minutes), close to beaches, whaling station, and wind farm; slightly out of town providing peace and quiet while remaining accessible to all attractions.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Facilities are significantly dated with insufficient toilet/shower numbers (only 3-4 showers total) causing queues and complaints, and the camp kitchen described as "dirty," "disgusting," and "basic" by multiple reviewers.
2. Zero dedicated children's entertainment or facilities (no playground, no activities, no kids club) making this unsuitable for families seeking structured entertainment for young children.
3. Operational and management issues including booking system problems, permanent residents creating an atmosphere that detracts from tourist experience, dog management problems, and inconsistent service quality.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"right on the water," "amazing views across the bay," "super friendly and helpful staff," "walked us to our site," "facilities are dated but clean," "huge camp sites," "close to Torndirrup National Park," "great value for money," "spotless and well maintained," "only 4 showers for all campers," "camp kitchen was the dirtiest," "peaceful waterfront location"

rationale_top3: Exceptional cleanliness standards with spotless amenities cleaned 4 times daily and immaculately manicured grounds that reviewers consistently rate as the best maintained park they've experienced Outstanding natural riverfront setting on the King River with abundant wildlife, kayak/paddleboard launch access, spacious grassy sites, and peaceful atmosphere just 10 minutes from Albany Strong location value combining tranquil riverside environment with convenient access to Albany beaches, shops and attractions, plus King River Tavern next door
rationale_honourable: Albany Happy Days Caravan Park earns Bronze recognition primarily for its exceptional maintenance standards and stunning natural riverfront setting. While it lacks dedicated children's entertainment facilities that families with young kids typically seek, the park compensates with immaculate grounds, spotless amenities cleaned multiple times daily, and spacious grassy sites along the King River where families can fish, kayak, and enjoy nature activities together.
water_fun: River access for kayaking and fishing
kids_play: No dedicated playground; nature-based activities only
pet_detail: Pet friendly park with dogs welcome on sites
best_for: Families with school-aged children who enjoy outdoor activities like fishing, kayaking, and bike riding in a pristine natural environment rather than organized entertainment
executive_summary:
PARK: Albany Happy Days Caravan Park
SCORE: 64/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-24
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 125

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (8/20) — Reviews consistently note this park has no dedicated kids' entertainment, playground, or waterpark facilities. One reviewer explicitly stated "It's not ideal for kids" while another defending the park mentioned families were criticized for children making noise. Positive family reviews focus on self-directed activities like "fishing in the river," "ride bikes around," and kayaking, indicating families must create their own entertainment.

Nature (18/20) — Exceptional natural environment with the park positioned "on the King River" providing "amazing river views." Multiple reviews mention "plenty of trees and birds around," "watching the kangaroos across the creek at sunset," and the ability to launch "kayaks and paddle boards right outside our site." Located "10km from town" offering peace while maintaining access to Albany's beaches and attractions.

Cleanliness (15/15) — Outstanding performance with reviews consistently calling it the "cleanest and best maintained caravan park" with "spotless amenities" cleaned "4 times a day." Reviewers note "immaculately kept park," "manicured lawns like a golf course," and facilities that are "cleaner than hotels." This is the park's strongest attribute across all reviews.

Value (12/15) — Good value mentioned at "$45 per night" and "very good price," though some reviews note billing issues with being "charged extra not once but twice." Generally considered reasonable for the quality provided, particularly given the exceptional maintenance standards, though not outstanding value compared to budget options.

Site Size (9/10) — Reviews consistently praise "spacious and level sites," "big sites," "large grassy sites," and "bays are so large, so you are not on top of each other." Sites feature "slab and green grass" with "plenty of space" for families. Minor deduction as "Site 1 & 2 only suitable for small van or camper trailers."

Sentiment (9/10) — Strong positive sentiment with many "will definitely return" comments and repeat visitors. However, significant concerning reviews about management including "appalling management and customer service," "staff are horrible," "owner was rude," and disputes over bookings and noise policies impact overall sentiment for families.

Location (10/10) — Perfect location score with park situated "only 11 mins drive from town," "10 minutes out of Albany," providing quiet riverside setting while maintaining easy access to "beaches and Albany town, shops." King River Tavern "right next door" and described as "close to everything" while being "far enough away to enjoy the starry skies."

WHY NOT 100:
1. Complete absence of on-site children's entertainment facilities (playground, jumping pillow, games room, kids' club) which is critical for family holiday parks catering to young children.
2. Multiple serious complaints about management customer service including families with young children being told the park "couldn't care less if you, or your children, lived or died" and disputes over quiet time enforcement even before 10pm official hours.
3. Some operational inconsistencies including booking disputes ("gave the site to a person who just drove up"), billing errors ("charged extra not once but twice"), and mixed messages about family-friendliness versus enforcing strict quiet policies.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"Cleanest and best maintained caravan park I've stayed at", "Camped here with my family", "Kids were welcome and could ride bikes around and had a great time fishing in the river", "It's not ideal for kids", "spotless amenities", "told off for laughing, giggling and talking around 9:10 pm", "beautiful, quiet camp ground on the river", "Very good value at $45 per night"

executive_summary:
PARK: Rose Gardens Holiday Park
SCORE: 38/100
DATE SCORED: 2024-01-15
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 111

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (4/20) — Reviews mention a basic playground for kids that is described as "tired and dated" with equipment needing attention. The games room is repeatedly criticized as "disgustingly dirty and neglected" with "spider webs, broken furniture" and no maintenance. No organized activities, movie nights, or entertainment programs were mentioned. Multiple families specifically warned others to "go to the big4 next door" if travelling with children.

Nature (16/20) — The park's standout feature is its exceptional oceanfront location at Emu Point with "unobstructed ocean views," direct beach access to Middleton Beach and Frenchman's Bay, and "beautiful birds" throughout the grounds. Families appreciated the "calm, shallow waters ideal for young children" and dog off-leash beach access. Walking and bike paths along the coast received consistent praise. The natural setting with rabbits and coastal environment scored well despite site maintenance issues.

Cleanliness (2/15) — This was the park's most severe failure point. Recent reviews (2024-2026) overwhelmingly describe "absolutely disgusting" and "revolting" amenities with "faeces on walls," "soap scum," "mould in showers," and facilities "not cleaned properly in a while." Multiple guests reported toilets cleaned "only once a day for 5 minutes" or going "14 nights with only 2 cleanings." The widespread ant infestation affecting "multiple tents and caravans" was a recurring complaint with management appearing unresponsive.

Value (8/15) — Reviews show mixed value perception. Some praised "exceptional value for money" at $54/night compared to Big 4 at $130/night, calling it "reasonably priced." However, many felt the poor condition didn't justify the cost, describing it as "overpriced" for the amenities provided. Families paying $40-60/night during peak periods felt "there is absolutely no excuse for amenities this dirty" given the revenue generated.

Site Size (6/10) — Reviews describe "large spacious sites" and "generous" site sizes with some concrete slabs available. However, many complained about "tiny sites," uneven ground "full of holes from rabbits," sandy sites instead of grass, and poor site allocation with guests "squeezed into the worst three spots" when the park was empty. Some sites were difficult to access and manoeuvre caravans into.

Sentiment (3/10) — Overall sentiment was predominantly negative in recent reviews with many stating "never going back," "wouldn't wish the experience on my worst enemy," and "worst caravan park I've ever stayed in." Multiple long-term repeat visitors said "this will be our last" stay. The few positive reviews praised location only. Evidence of management changes (RAC takeover pending) and owner disengagement contributed to negative sentiment.

Location (9/10) — The location at Emu Point received universal praise as "unbeatable," "perfect," and "the only good thing about this park." Guests valued being "right on the water," 10 minutes from Albany city centre, 30 minutes to The Gap and Natural Bridge, with easy access to beaches, coastal walks, and local cafe. The oceanfront position was consistently the highest-rated aspect.

WHY NOT 100:
The park suffers from catastrophic cleanliness failures with amenities described as "absolutely disgusting," "beyond filthy," and health hazards, with inadequate cleaning schedules and a severe ant infestation that management failed to address despite widespread complaints affecting families with young children. Facilities and infrastructure are severely dated, run-down and neglected with broken equipment, derelict buildings, no maintenance between guests, blocked dump points, non-functional dryers, and a recreation room with "spider webs and broken furniture" creating an unsafe environment for children. Customer service and management response was repeatedly criticized as "extremely rude," "dismissive," and showing complete lack of care, with reports of shouting at guests, refusing refunds despite serious issues, and failing to address safety concerns including multiple thefts with no security measures in place.

"worst showers of all", "amenities are revolting", "ants everywhere", "absolutely disgusting", "filthy amenities", "wouldn't bring kids", "location is the best thing", "management that didn't care", "games room hasn't been cleaned in months", "we were robbed", "exceptional value for money", "beautiful Emu Point Beach"
